defs: A stout, coarse shoe; a brogan.
notes: ☞ In the Highlands of Scotland, the ancient brogue was made of horsehide or deerskin, untanned or tenned with the hair on, gathered round the ankle with a thong. The name was afterward given to any shoe worn as a part of the Highland costume.

defs: A dialectic pronunciation; esp. the Irish manner of pronouncing English.
quotes: Or take, Hibernis, thy still ranker brogue.
